parameters.documentation: STRING variable to receive the chosen font name.

parameters.label: size
parameters.documentation: Optional. SIGNED variable to receive the point size.

parameters.label: color
parameters.documentation: Optional. LONG variable to receive the RGB color.

parameters.label: style
parameters.documentation: Optional. LONG variable to receive the style flags.

parameters.label: charset
parameters.documentation: Optional. BYTE variable to receive the character set value.

parameters.label: added
parameters.documentation: Optional. 0=screen fonts, 1=printer fonts, 2=both, 4=TrueType only, 8=fixed pitch only.

functions.name: GETEXITCODE
functions.description: Gets the program exit code (error level) without closing the program. Useful for displaying a meaningful message or taking action before termination.

signatures.label: GETEXITCODE( )
signatures.documentation: Returns the current program exit code (same value that would be passed to the OS on termination).

functions.name: LONGPATH
functions.description: Returns the fully-qualified long filename for a given short (8.3) filename. The file must already exist on disk. With no parameter, returns the current drive and directory in long name form.

signatures.label: LONGPATH( [ shortfilename ] )
signatures.documentation:
Converts a short filename to its long filename form.
- **shortfilename** *(optional)* — DOS 8.3 filename to convert (may include full path). Omit to get the current directory.

Returns: `STRING`

parameters.label: shortfilename
parameters.documentation: Optional. DOS short (8.3) filename/path to convert. Omit for current directory.

functions.name: NOMEMO
functions.description: Arms 'memoless' record retrieval for the next GET, REGET, NEXT, or PREVIOUS statement. The record is retrieved without reading MEMO or BLOB fields, improving performance when those fields are not needed.

signatures.label: NOMEMO( file )
signatures.documentation:
Arms memoless retrieval for the next GET/NEXT/PREVIOUS/REGET.
- **file** — label of the FILE to read without its MEMO/BLOB fields.

parameters.label: file
parameters.documentation: Label of the FILE to arm for memoless retrieval.

functions.name: NOTIFICATION
functions.description: Receives information sent by NOTIFY from another thread. Returns TRUE if the current event is EVENT:Notify; otherwise returns FALSE. Used in the receiving thread alongside an ACCEPT loop.

signatures.label: NOTIFICATION( notifycode, <thread>, <parameter> )
signatures.documentation:
Receives a notification posted by NOTIFY from another thread.
- **notifycode** — UNSIGNED variable to receive the notify code.
- **thread** *(optional)* — SIGNED variable to receive the sender's thread number.
- **parameter** *(optional)* — LONG variable to receive the parameter passed by NOTIFY.

Returns: `BYTE` (TRUE = EVENT:Notify was received; FALSE = other event)

parameters.label: notifycode
parameters.documentation: UNSIGNED variable to receive the notification code.

parameters.label: thread
parameters.documentation: Optional. SIGNED variable to receive the sender thread number.

parameters.label: parameter
parameters.documentation: Optional. LONG variable to receive the parameter from NOTIFY.

functions.name: NOTIFY
functions.description: Sends an EVENT:Notify event and optional parameters to a receiver thread's event queue. Execution continues immediately without waiting for the receiver. A safe replacement for SETTARGET(,thread).

signatures.label: NOTIFY( notifycode [, thread ] [, parameter ] )
signatures.documentation:
Sends a notification event to a receiver thread.
- **notifycode** — unsigned integer notification/request code.
- **thread** *(optional)* — SIGNED thread number; 0 or omitted = current thread.
- **parameter** *(optional)* — LONG value passed to the receiver as additional data.

parameters.label: notifycode
parameters.documentation: Unsigned integer notification code to send.

parameters.label: thread
parameters.documentation: Optional. Thread number of the receiver; 0 or omitted = current thread.

parameters.label: parameter
parameters.documentation: Optional. LONG value to pass to the receiver.

functions.name: PACK
functions.description: Removes deleted records from a data file and rebuilds its keys, producing the most compact file possible. Requires exclusive access (mode 12h or 22h). Generates EVENT:BuildFile, EVENT:BuildKey, and EVENT:BuildDone events if PROP:ProgressEvents is set.

signatures.label: PACK( file )
signatures.documentation:
Removes deleted records and rebuilds keys for a FILE.
- **file** — label of a FILE declaration.

Errors posted: 63 (Exclusive Access Required)
Events generated: EVENT:BuildFile, EVENT:BuildKey, EVENT:BuildDone

parameters.label: file
parameters.documentation: Label of the FILE to pack.

functions.name: PRINT
functions.description: Prints a DETAIL report structure. PRINT automatically activates group breaks and page overflow as needed. The REPORT must be OPENed before use.

signatures.label: PRINT( structure | report, number )
signatures.documentation:
Prints a DETAIL structure within an open REPORT.
- **structure** — label of a DETAIL structure, OR
- **report** — label of the REPORT, **number** — field equate label of the DETAIL structure to print.

parameters.label: structure
parameters.documentation: Label of a DETAIL structure to print; or use report + number form.

parameters.label: report
parameters.documentation: Label of the REPORT (use with number parameter).

parameters.label: number
parameters.documentation: Field equate label of the DETAIL structure within the report.

functions.name: RELEASE
functions.description: Releases a record previously held by HOLD. Has no effect if the record is not held by the current user. Used in multi-user environments to release locks after processing.

signatures.label: RELEASE( entity )
signatures.documentation:
Releases a held record lock.
- **entity** — label of a FILE or VIEW declaration.

parameters.label: entity
parameters.documentation: Label of the FILE or VIEW whose held record should be released.

functions.name: RESUME
functions.description: Resumes execution of a thread suspended with SUSPEND. Each RESUME decrements the suspend count; execution only resumes when the count reaches zero. Can also be used to activate a new thread immediately upon start.

signatures.label: RESUME( threadno )
signatures.documentation:
Resumes a suspended thread.
- **threadno** — SIGNED integer thread number to resume.

Example: `RESUME(START(MyProc))` — starts a new thread immediately.

parameters.label: threadno
parameters.documentation: Thread number to resume (must match a previous SUSPEND call).

functions.name: RIGHT
functions.description: Returns a right-justified string. Trailing spaces are removed, then the string is right-padded with leading spaces to the specified length.

signatures.label: RIGHT( string [, length ] )
signatures.documentation:
Right-justifies a string.
- **string** — string constant, variable, or expression.
- **length** *(optional)* — result length in characters. Defaults to the length of `string`.

Returns: `STRING`

parameters.label: string
parameters.documentation: String to right-justify.

parameters.label: length
parameters.documentation: Optional. Length of the returned string. Defaults to length of the input string.

functions.name: ROLLBACK
functions.description: Terminates an active transaction by restoring the database to the state it was in before the transaction began. Use after detecting an error inside a LOGOUT block.

signatures.label: ROLLBACK
signatures.documentation:
Rolls back the active transaction (started with LOGOUT).

Errors posted: 65 (Unable to ROLLBACK Transaction), 91 (No Logout Active).

functions.name: RUN
functions.description: Executes a DOS or Windows command. If `waitflag` is 0 (default) control returns immediately; if 1 control waits for the command to finish. Wrap STRING variables with CLIP to remove trailing spaces.

signatures.label: RUN( command [, waitflag ] )
signatures.documentation:
Executes a command or program.
- **command** — string with the program/command and any parameters. Use CLIP if a STRING variable.
- **waitflag** *(optional)* — 0 (default) = return immediately; 1 = wait for completion.

Use RUNCODE to check the exit code after completion.

parameters.label: command
parameters.documentation: String with the program name and parameters. Use CLIP for STRING variables.

parameters.label: waitflag
parameters.documentation: Optional. 0 = return immediately (default); 1 = wait for program to exit.

functions.name: RUNCODE
functions.description: Returns the exit code passed to the OS by the program last executed with RUN. Negative values indicate special conditions: -1 = Ctrl-C, -2 = Critical Error, -3 = TSR exit, -4 = program did not run.

signatures.label: RUNCODE( )
signatures.documentation:
Returns the exit code of the last RUN command.

Special values: 0=normal, -1=Ctrl-C, -2=Critical Error, -3=TSR exit, -4=did not run (check ERROR()).

Returns: `LONG`

functions.name: SEND
functions.description: Sends a driver-specific message string to a file driver at runtime. Valid messages depend on the file driver in use. Can also be used to send SQL statements to backend databases (though property syntax is preferred).

signatures.label: SEND( file, message )
signatures.documentation:
Sends a message to the file driver.
- **file** — label of the FILE whose DRIVER attribute identifies the target driver.
- **message** — string containing driver-specific information.

Returns: `STRING` (driver response, if any)

parameters.label: file
parameters.documentation: Label of the FILE declaration.

parameters.label: message
parameters.documentation: Driver-specific message string (see driver documentation for valid values).

functions.name: SETCLIPBOARD
functions.description: Places a string into the Windows clipboard (CF_TEXT format), overwriting any previous contents. The string should not contain embedded null characters.

signatures.label: SETCLIPBOARD( string )
signatures.documentation:
Sets the Windows clipboard contents to the given string.
- **string** — string constant or variable to place in the clipboard (CF_TEXT format only).

parameters.label: string
parameters.documentation: String to place in the Windows clipboard.

functions.name: SETCLOCK
functions.description: Sets the operating system time of day. The time value is expressed as hundredths of a second since midnight, plus one (Clarion standard time format).

signatures.label: SETCLOCK( time )
signatures.documentation:
Sets the system clock.
- **time** — standard Clarion time value (hundredths of a second since midnight, plus 1). Example: 1 = midnight, 6001 = one minute past midnight.

parameters.label: time
parameters.documentation: Clarion standard time (hundredths of a second since midnight + 1).

functions.name: SETCOMMAND
functions.description: Internally sets command-line parameters that can be read by COMMAND. Overwrites any existing flag of the same name. Append `=` to a `/flag` to turn it off. Cannot set system-level switches that must be specified at load time.

signatures.label: SETCOMMAND( commandline )
signatures.documentation:
Sets internal command-line parameters.
- **commandline** — string with the parameters to add/override (e.g. `' /N'` to add, `' /N='` to remove).

parameters.label: commandline
parameters.documentation: String of parameters to set (e.g. ' /N' to add, ' /N=' to remove).

functions.name: SETCURSOR
functions.description: Specifies a temporary mouse cursor that overrides all CURSOR attributes until a SETCURSOR with no parameter turns it off. Typically used to show an hourglass during long operations.

signatures.label: SETCURSOR( [ cursor ] )
signatures.documentation:
Sets or clears a temporary mouse cursor.
- **cursor** *(optional)* — EQUATE for a Windows-standard cursor (e.g. `CURSOR:Wait`), or a string naming a linked .CUR resource (prefix with `~`). Omit to restore normal cursors.

Cursor EQUATEs are in EQUATES.CLW.

parameters.label: cursor
parameters.documentation: Optional. CURSOR: equate or '~Filename.CUR' string. Omit to turn off temporary cursor.

functions.name: SETFONT
functions.description: Dynamically sets the display font for a control or window, overriding any FONT attribute.

signatures.label: SETFONT(control|window [, typeface] [, size] [, color] [, style] [, charset])
signatures.documentation: Sets the display font for a control (field equate or 0 for window) or a named window/report structure. typeface is a string font name; size is in points; color is a LONG RGB value; style combines FONT:thin/regular/bold/italic/underline/strikeout equates; charset is the character set value. SETFONT changes all parameters at once rather than one at a time. If called before a window is opened, the FONT attribute is completely ignored.

parameters.label: control|window
parameters.documentation: A field number, field equate label (or 0 for window), or the label of a WINDOW/APPLICATION/REPORT structure.

parameters.label: typeface
parameters.documentation: Optional. String constant or variable containing the font name. Omit to use the system font.

parameters.label: size
parameters.documentation: Optional. Integer point size. Omit to use the system default.

parameters.label: color
parameters.documentation: Optional. LONG integer with RGB color in the low-order three bytes.

parameters.label: style
parameters.documentation: Optional. Integer combining FONT:thin (100), FONT:regular (400), FONT:bold (700), FONT:italic, FONT:underline, FONT:strikeout equates.

parameters.label: charset
parameters.documentation: Optional. LONG integer specifying the Windows character set value.

functions.name: SETPOSITION
functions.description: Dynamically specifies the position and size of an APPLICATION, WINDOW, REPORT, or control.

signatures.label: SETPOSITION(control|target [, x] [, y] [, width] [, height])
signatures.documentation: Sets the position and/or dimensions of a control or window. control is a field number/equate (0 for the current window) or the label of an APPLICATION/WINDOW/REPORT. x and y are the top-left corner position; width and height are the dimensions — all measured in dialog units. Omitting any parameter leaves that value unchanged. SETPOSITION changes all parameters at once, avoiding the visible 'jump' that occurs with individual property assignments.

parameters.label: control|target
parameters.documentation: Field number, field equate label (0 = current window), or the label of an APPLICATION, WINDOW, or REPORT structure.

parameters.label: x
parameters.documentation: Optional. Horizontal position of the top-left corner in dialog units.

parameters.label: y
parameters.documentation: Optional. Vertical position of the top-left corner in dialog units.

parameters.label: width
parameters.documentation: Optional. Width in dialog units.

parameters.label: height
parameters.documentation: Optional. Height in dialog units.

functions.name: SHORTPATH
functions.description: Returns the fully-qualified short (8.3) filename for a given long filename.

signatures.label: SHORTPATH([longfilename])
signatures.documentation: Returns the DOS standard short filename for the given longfilename. The file must exist on disk. If longfilename is omitted, returns the current drive and directory in short name form. If 8.3 name creation is disabled on NTFS, SHORTPATH and PATH return the same value. Can be used in expressions evaluated by the EVALUATE statement.

parameters.label: longfilename
parameters.documentation: Optional. String constant, variable, or expression specifying the long filename to convert, including the full path.

functions.name: SUSPEND
functions.description: Suspends the execution of a specified thread.

signatures.label: SUSPEND(threadno, quietmode)
signatures.documentation: Suspends the thread identified by threadno. Each SUSPEND increments the thread's suspend counter; an equal number of RESUME calls is needed to resume execution. quietmode controls messaging: TRUE (1) suppresses the warning message if the suspension is unsafe; FALSE (0) displays the message. Returns TRUE (1) if the thread was suspended, FALSE (0) if it could not be suspended. Suspending an inactive thread is ignored. Improper use in MDI programs can cause lockups.

parameters.label: threadno
parameters.documentation: A numeric constant, variable, or expression (SIGNED) identifying the thread to suspend.

parameters.label: quietmode
parameters.documentation: A numeric constant, variable, or expression (SIGNED): TRUE (1) suppresses the unsafe-suspend message; FALSE (0) displays it.

functions.name: THREAD
functions.description: Returns the number of the currently executing thread.

signatures.label: THREAD()
signatures.documentation: Returns the currently executing thread number as a SIGNED integer (1 to 2,147,483,647). The returned value can be used with procedures such as SETTARGET and START that operate on specific execution threads.

functions.name: WATCH
functions.description: Arms automatic optimistic concurrency checking for a FILE or VIEW.

signatures.label: WATCH(entity)
signatures.documentation: Arms automatic optimistic concurrency checking for the specified FILE or VIEW. After WATCH, the file driver retains a copy of the retrieved record. When the record is PUT back, the copy on disk is compared against the original. If another user has changed the record, PUT posts Error 89 (Record Changed By Another Station). WATCH terminates when the record is PUT or another GET/NEXT/PREVIOUS/REGET executes without a preceding WATCH.

parameters.label: entity
parameters.documentation: The label of a FILE or VIEW declaration to arm for concurrency checking.

functions.name: SETTARGET
functions.description: Sets the current window or report as the target for runtime property assignment and drawing statements.

signatures.label: SETTARGET([target] [, thread|band])
signatures.documentation: Sets the built-in TARGET variable used by CREATE, SETPOSITION, GETPOSITION, SETFONT, GETFONT, DISABLE, HIDE, CONTENTS, DISPLAY, ERASE, UPDATE, and graphics primitives. target is the label or reference of an APPLICATION, WINDOW, or REPORT; thread selects the topmost window in that execution thread; band selects a REPORT band or IMAGE control for graphics drawing. SETTARGET() with no arguments resets to the active ACCEPT loop's procedure and thread. A REPORT is never the default TARGET — always call SETTARGET before drawing on a REPORT.

parameters.label: target
parameters.documentation: Optional. Label or reference of an APPLICATION, WINDOW, or REPORT structure. Omit to use the topmost window in the specified thread.

parameters.label: thread|band
parameters.documentation: Optional. The execution thread number (when target is omitted), or the field equate label of a REPORT band or IMAGE control (for graphics drawing).

functions.name: SETEXITCODE
functions.description: Sets the program exit code (DOS ERRORLEVEL) returned when the application terminates.

signatures.label: SETEXITCODE(exitcode)
signatures.documentation: Sets the program exit code passed to the operating system (DOS ERRORLEVEL) when the application ends. exitcode is a positive integer constant or variable. If omitted, the default is zero. Useful for signalling meaningful exit status to calling scripts or programs without having to close the application immediately.

parameters.label: exitcode
parameters.documentation: Optional. A positive integer constant or variable specifying the exit code (ERRORLEVEL) to return.

functions.name: POPERRORS
functions.description: Restores error information previously saved by PUSHERRORS.

signatures.label: POPERRORS()
signatures.documentation: Restores the last error state saved by PUSHERRORS, including ERRORCODE(), ERROR(), FILEERRORCODE(), FILEERROR(), ERRORFILE(), the C library errno, and the Windows GetLastError() code. Works on a LIFO stack with no depth limit other than available memory. Error state is thread-specific; POPERRORS from different threads operate on independent stacks.

functions.name: PUSHERRORS
functions.description: Saves the current error information to an internal stack for later retrieval with POPERRORS.

signatures.label: PUSHERRORS()
signatures.documentation: Saves the current thread's error states — ERRORCODE(), ERROR(), FILEERRORCODE(), FILEERROR(), ERRORFILE(), the C library errno, and the Windows GetLastError() code — to a LIFO stack. Use POPERRORS to restore them. Useful to preserve error state before performing additional code that might change it. Error state is thread-specific; PUSHERRORS from different threads operate on independent stacks.

functions.name: PUSHBIND
functions.description: Creates a new scope for subsequent BIND statements, saving the current name space.

signatures.label: PUSHBIND([clearflag])
signatures.documentation: Creates a new BIND name space scope. clearflag = 0 (default) clears all previously bound variable and procedure names; clearflag = 1 leaves them in place. The scope is terminated by the corresponding POPBIND. Useful when calling code that uses the same variable names as the outer scope, preventing name conflicts in runtime expression strings.

parameters.label: clearflag
parameters.documentation: Optional. 0 (default) clears the BIND name space; 1 preserves existing bindings in the new scope.

functions.name: POPBIND
functions.description: Restores the BIND name space to the state before the last PUSHBIND.

signatures.label: POPBIND
signatures.documentation: Restores the previous BIND statement name space, undoing the scope created by PUSHBIND. Variable and procedure names that were bound before PUSHBIND are accessible again. PUSHBIND and POPBIND work as a LIFO stack.

functions.name: BINDEXPRESSION
functions.description: Pre-compiles a runtime expression and binds it to a name for efficient repeated evaluation.

signatures.label: BINDEXPRESSION(name, expression)
signatures.documentation: Parses and pre-compiles the expression string once, storing the result under name. The named expression can then be used in EVALUATE or in other BINDEXPRESSION calls without re-parsing. This is more efficient than repeated EVALUATE calls when the same expression is evaluated frequently. All variables referenced in expression must first be bound with BIND.

parameters.label: name
parameters.documentation: A string constant containing the identifier to assign to the pre-compiled expression.

parameters.label: expression
parameters.documentation: A string containing a valid Clarion language logical or mathematical expression, which may reference BINDed variables or other named BINDEXPRESSION expressions.

functions.name: LOCALE
functions.description: Loads a locale environment file (.ENV) or sets individual locale/environment settings at runtime.

signatures.label: LOCALE(file|setting [, value])
signatures.documentation: Loads a named .ENV file (or the keyword WINDOWS for Windows defaults) to override locale settings such as collating sequence, case mappings, date/time formats, and button labels. Alternatively, sets individual environment variables by specifying setting (e.g. 'CLACODEPAGE', 'CLALCID') and value. Errors posted: 02 File Not Found, 05 Access Denied.

parameters.label: file|setting
parameters.documentation: String constant or variable: the .ENV filename to load, the keyword WINDOWS, or the name of an individual environment variable to set (e.g. 'CLACODEPAGE', 'CLALCID', 'CLABUTTON').

parameters.label: value
parameters.documentation: Optional. The value to assign when setting an individual environment variable.

functions.name: THREADLOCKED
functions.description: Returns the locked/unlocked state of the current execution thread.

signatures.label: THREADLOCKED()
signatures.documentation: Returns 1 if the current execution thread is locked (the normal state inside an ACCEPT loop), or 0 if it has been unlocked by UNLOCKTHREAD. Use before calling LOCKTHREAD or UNLOCKTHREAD to check the current state.

functions.name: LOCKTHREAD
functions.description: Re-locks the current execution thread after it was unlocked with UNLOCKTHREAD.

signatures.label: LOCKTHREAD
signatures.documentation: Re-locks the current execution thread that was previously unlocked with UNLOCKTHREAD. Must be called after the external (non-Clarion) procedure that required thread unlocking returns. Between UNLOCKTHREAD and LOCKTHREAD, no Clarion runtime library calls are permitted.

functions.name: UNLOCKTHREAD
functions.description: Unlocks the current execution thread to allow other threads to execute while calling external code with its own message loop.

signatures.label: UNLOCKTHREAD
signatures.documentation: Unlocks the current execution thread so that other threads can run cooperatively while the current thread calls external (non-Clarion) code that has its own message loop. After the external call returns, use LOCKTHREAD to re-lock the thread. Between UNLOCKTHREAD and LOCKTHREAD, you must NOT call any Clarion runtime procedure or perform operations on STRING, CSTRING, PSTRING, DECIMAL, or PDECIMAL types.

functions.name: INSTANCE
functions.description: Returns the address of a variable's thread-specific instance.

signatures.label: INSTANCE(variable, threadno)
signatures.documentation: Returns the address of the thread instance of variable allocated for the thread identified by threadno. If threadno is 0, returns the thread-independent ID (the address assigned by the program loader). If threadno is non-zero and that thread is not active or has no instance of variable, returns 0. Preferred over ADDRESS() for FILE and QUEUE structures, and for obtaining thread-independent IDs of threaded variables.

parameters.label: variable
parameters.documentation: Label of a variable, field, FILE, KEY, QUEUE, or reference variable.

parameters.label: threadno
parameters.documentation: A numeric constant, variable, or expression (SIGNED) identifying the thread. Use 0 to get the thread-independent ID.

signatures.description: Hides one or more window controls at runtime. With no parameters, hides all controls. Using first alone hides that control; first and last hides an inclusive range of controls by USE order. Controls remain in memory but are not visible. Use UNHIDE to make them visible again.

Dims (grays out) one or more window controls at runtime, preventing user interaction. With no parameters, disables all controls. Using first alone disables that control; first and last disables an inclusive range by USE order. Use ENABLE to re-activate.
In a REPORT structure, writes a string to the current print position. Used to output dynamically formatted text at report generation time.

Calls a procedure from a Windows standard .DLL that has not been prototyped in the MAP structure. The DLL is loaded if not already resident. Returns 0 on success, or a Windows error code on failure. The DLL is unloaded when the procedure finishes unless flags bit 0 is set to 1. Use UNLOAD to explicitly unload a resident DLL.

Registers or unregisters a FileCallBackInterface with a FILE or VIEW. The interface methods are called automatically before (FunctionCalled) and after (FunctionDone) each file operation. Multiple interfaces can be registered. Set flag to TRUE (1) to unregister. Registered interfaces must be unregistered before the implementing object is destroyed.

Translates a string or GROUP from the ANSI (Windows display) character set into the OEM character set (ASCII with extra characters defined by the active code page). Not required when the OEM file attribute is set. Companion to CONVERTOEMTOANSI.

Translates a string or GROUP from the OEM character set (ASCII with extra characters defined by the active code page) into the ANSI (Windows display) character set. Not required when the OEM file attribute is set. Companion to CONVERTANSITOOEM.

Returns the current horizontal position of the mouse cursor at the time of the event, relative to the origin of the active window. Returns dialog units by default; returns pixels if PROP:Pixels is set to TRUE.

Returns the current vertical position of the mouse cursor at the time of the event, relative to the origin of the active window. Returns dialog units by default; returns pixels if PROP:Pixels is set to TRUE.

Writes the contents of a variable to a memory address. Writes as many bytes as are in the source variable. Use ADDRESS() to obtain a safe protected-mode address. Incorrect use can cause General Protection Faults. Prefer Windows API functions over POKE where possible.

Displays the Windows standard printer choice dialog. flag: 0=Choose Printer, 1=Print Setup, 2=Page Setup. Sets the default printer for the next REPORT opened. Returns 1 if user clicked OK, 0 if Cancel.

Registers an event handler procedure to be called by the ACCEPT loop whenever the specified event occurs. handler is the ADDRESS of the procedure. object is a unique 32-bit identifier (typically ADDRESS(SELF)). Multiple handlers can be registered for the same event; they execute in reverse registration order. Can also be prototyped as REGISTEREVENT.

Unregisters a previously REGISTERed event handler. Omitting parameters unregisters all matching handlers (e.g., omit event to unregister all events for the object). The runtime automatically unregisters all handlers when a window closes, so explicit UNREGISTER is only needed for early deregistration. Can also be prototyped as UNREGISTEREVENT.

Toggles the three-dimensional look and feel for window controls. SET3DLOOK(0) disables 3D look; SET3DLOOK(1) enables it (default). The GRAY attribute on a WINDOW causes controls to appear three-dimensional. TOOLBAR controls are always 3D unless disabled by SET3DLOOK.

Sets the value that DROPID() will return, allowing data to be passed in a drag-and-drop operation. Used in EVENT:Drag processing to package the dragged data for the drop target, especially when passing data between separate Clarion applications.
Sets the internal ASCII character value returned by the KEYCHAR procedure. The character is not placed in the keyboard buffer. Used to override the character returned from the last keystroke event.

Sets the keycode value returned by the KEYCODE procedure. The keycode is not placed in the keyboard buffer. Used to override the keycode returned from the last keystroke event. Accepts integer constants or keycode EQUATE labels.

Changes the current drive and directory. If the drive and path are invalid, posts error 03 (Path Not Found). If only a drive letter and colon are specified, changes to the current directory of that drive. Companion to PATH, SHORTPATH, LONGPATH.
Sets the operating system date. date is a standard Clarion date numeric value. Use TODAY() to get the current date and arithmetic to calculate new dates.

Bypasses records during sequential processing by moving the file pointer count records without loading data into the record buffer. More efficient than repeated NEXT/PREVIOUS calls. Positive count skips forward; negative count skips backward. Posts errors if past end/beginning of file or if SET has not been issued.

Specifies a native SQL expression for use in a VIEW FILTER, ORDER, or JOIN, or in PROP:Filter, PROP:Order, PROP:JoinExpression. Anything inside SQL() is passed directly to the SQL server without Clarion translation. The SQL driver generates aliases (A, B, etc.) for each file in a VIEW; use those aliases in your SQL expressions.

Registers or unregisters a SQLCallBackInterface with a FILE or VIEW. The interface's ExecutingCode method is called just before each SQL statement is sent to the SQL server. Set flag to TRUE (1) to unregister. Only supported by SQL file drivers.
Returns the square root of a numeric expression. If the value is less than zero, returns zero. The square root of X is the number that, when multiplied by itself, equals X.

Disables automatic file buffer flushing for a FILE, allowing the OS to buffer writes. Terminated by CLOSE (which flushes automatically) or FLUSH. Use in single-user or batch contexts; LOGOUT is safer in multi-user environments. Support is file driver dependent.

Associates a numeric value with an ASTRING token and index. If index is omitted or zero, a unique index is allocated at runtime and returned. Used with TIED and UNTIE to attach metadata to ASTRING values.
Retrieves a numeric value previously associated with an ASTRING token and index using TIE. Returns 0 if no value has been tied for that token and index.
Disassociates a value previously tied to an ASTRING token and index using TIE. If stringtoken is omitted, all ASTRINGs are affected. If index is omitted, all indexes for the token are untied.
Unloads a Windows standard .DLL that was left resident by CALL with flags bit 0 set. Use when the DLL is no longer needed to free memory.
